---
title: "FFCWS Data Cleaning Secondary School Data"
output:
  word_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```
# SETUP

## load libraries
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(gtsummary)
library(mice)
library(sjPlot)
library(labelled)
library(psych)
```

## load data
```{r}
start_time <- Sys.time()

# load dataset
df_raw <- haven::read_dta("ffcws.dta")

# calc time to load dataset
end_time <- Sys.time()
end_time - start_time

# Convert all column names to lowercase
names(df_raw) <- tolower(names(df_raw))
```

## replace missing values
```{r}
df_na <- df_raw

start_time <- Sys.time()
df_na[df_na==-9] <- NA # not in wave
df_na[df_na==-8] <- NA # out of range
df_na[df_na==-7] <- NA # n/a
df_na[df_na==-6] <- NA # skip
df_na[df_na==-5] <- NA # not asked
df_na[df_na==-4] <- NA # multiple answer
df_na[df_na==-3] <- NA # missing
df_na[df_na==-2] <- NA # don't know
df_na[df_na==-1] <- NA # refuse
end_time <- Sys.time()
end_time - start_time


df_na = df_na %>% drop_unused_value_labels()
#see new values
# view_df(df_na, show.values = TRUE, show.prc = TRUE, show.na = TRUE, sort.by.name = TRUE)
```

# Recode & Rename

## Make variable copies with new names
```{r}
df <- df_na %>%
  dplyr::mutate(

  ## math classes
    math_basic = k7b14_1  ,
    math_alg1 =k7b14_2  ,
    math_geom =k7b14_3  ,
    math_alg2 =k7b14_4  ,
    math_trig =k7b14_5  ,
    math_precal = k7b14_6  ,
    math_calc = k7b14_7  ,
    math_adv = k7b14_8  ,
    math_other = k7b14_9  ,
    math_none = k7b14_10 ,
    math_ap = k7b23_1  ,
  
  # demographics
    birth_gender = cm1bsex,
    birth_weight = cm1lbw,
    mom_age_y00 = cm1age,
    dad_age_y00 = cf1age,
    race_ethnicity = ck6ethrace,
    female = ifelse(birth_gender == 2,1,0),
    mom_US = ifelse(m1h2 == 1,1,0),
    mom_married = ifelse(m1b2 == 1,1,0),
  
  # using mom data
    poverty_category_y00 = cm1povca,
    poverty_ratio_y00 = cm1inpov,
    poverty_category_y01 = cm2povca,
    poverty_ratio_y01 = cm2povco,
    poverty_category_y03 = cm3povca,
    poverty_ratio_y03 = cm3povco,
    poverty_category_y05 = cm4povca,
    poverty_ratio_y05 = cm4povco,
    poverty_category_y09 = cm5povca,
    poverty_ratio_y09 = cm5povco,
  
  # Standardized Test scores
  
    pcg_ppvt_y03   = ch3ppvtstd_m,
    pcg_tvip_y03   = ch3tvipstd_m,
    child_ppvt_y03 = ch3ppvtstd,
    child_tvip_y03 = ch3tvipstd,
  
    pcg_ppvt_y05   = ch4ppvtstd,
    pcg_tvip_y05   = ch4tvipstd_m,
    child_ppvt_y05 = ch4ppvtstd,
    child_tvip_y05 = ch4tvipstd_m,
    child_ppvt_y09 = ch5ppvtss
  )

```


## create new math and race variables
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(haven)

df <- df %>%
  mutate(

    # ------------------
    # High school math (6-level)
    # ------------------
    high_math = case_when(
      is.na(math_none) ~ NA_real_,

      math_calc == 1 | math_ap == 1 ~ 5,
      math_precal == 1 | math_trig == 1 | math_adv == 1 ~ 4,
      math_alg2 == 1 ~ 3,
      math_geom == 1 ~ 2,
      math_alg1 == 1 ~ 1,
      math_basic == 1 | math_other == 1 | math_none == 1 ~ 0
    ),
  math_credits = math_alg1 + math_geom + math_alg2 + math_precal + math_calc,
    high_math = labelled(
      high_math,
      labels = c(
        "none or basic" = 0,
        "alg1" = 1,
        "geometry" = 2,
        "alg2" = 3,
        "trig, precalc/alg3 or other advanced math" = 4,
        "calc or AP math" = 5
      )
    ),

    # ------------------
    # Collapsed math level (4-level)
    # ------------------
    level_math = case_when(
      is.na(math_none) ~ NA_real_,

      math_calc == 1 | math_ap == 1 ~ 4,
      math_precal == 1 | math_trig == 1 | math_adv == 1 ~ 3,
      math_alg2 == 1 ~ 2,
      math_geom == 1 | math_alg1 == 1 | math_basic == 1 |
        math_other == 1 | math_none == 1 ~ 1
    ),

    level_math = labelled(
      level_math,
      labels = c(
        "geometry or below" = 1,
        "alg2" = 2,
        "trig, precalc/alg3 or other advanced math" = 3,
        "calc or AP math" = 4
      )
    )
  )


# ------------------
# Advanced math dummy
# ------------------
df <- df %>%
  mutate(adv_math = if_else(high_math >= 5, 1, 0))

# ------------------
# Effect coding (White = reference)
# race_ethnicity: 1 = White, 2 = Black, 3 = Latino, 4 = Another Race, 5 = Multiracial
# ------------------
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    black_ec = case_when(
      race_ethnicity == 2 ~  1,
      race_ethnicity == 1 ~ -1,
      TRUE                ~  0
    ),
    latinx_ec = case_when(
      race_ethnicity == 3 ~  1,
      race_ethnicity == 1 ~ -1,
      TRUE                ~  0
    ),
    another_ec = case_when(
      race_ethnicity == 4 ~  1,
      race_ethnicity == 1 ~ -1,
      TRUE                ~  0
    ),
    multi_ec = case_when(
      race_ethnicity == 5 ~  1,
      race_ethnicity == 1 ~ -1,
      TRUE                ~  0
    )
  )
  
```

# Create Validated Scales

## Scales for Xin Li Project
```{r}
df <- df %>% 
  mutate(
# ------------------
# ASBI
# ------------------
    asbi_scale_y03 = rowMeans(select(., p3m4, p3m8, p3m12, p3m15, p3m20, p3m24, p3m27, p3m34, p3m43), na.rm = FALSE),
    asbi_scale_y05 = rowMeans(select(., p4l3, p4l11, p4l15, p4l30, p4l32, p4l41, p4l48, p4l51, p4l55, p4l58, p4l60, p4l63, p4l66), na.rm = FALSE),
    asbi_scale_y09 = rowMeans(select(., p5q3dc:p5q3do), na.rm = FALSE),
    asbi_scale_y15 = rowMeans(select(., k6d1a:k6d1l), na.rm = FALSE),
    
# ------------------
# Parental Depression
# ------------------
    # using the constructed variable makes more sense here since the questions are different depending on the city. 
# coul also use na.rm = FALSE to account for this...we'll see
    mom_lib_dep_y01 = cm2md_case_lib,
    dad_lib_dep_y01 = cf2md_case_lib,
    mom_con_dep_y01 = cm2md_case_con,
    dad_con_dep_y01 = cf2md_case_con,
    mom_lib_dep_y03 = cm3md_case_lib,
    dad_lib_dep_y03 = cf3md_case_lib,
    mom_con_dep_y03 = cm3md_case_con,
    dad_con_dep_y03 = cf3md_case_con,
    mom_lib_dep_y05 = cm4md_case_lib,
    dad_lib_dep_y05 = cf4md_case_lib,
    mom_con_dep_y05 = cm4md_case_con,
    dad_con_dep_y05 = cf4md_case_con,
    mom_lib_dep_y09 = cm5md_case_lib,
    dad_lib_dep_y09 = cf5md_case_lib,
    mom_con_dep_y09 = cm5md_case_con,
    dad_con_dep_y09 = cf5md_case_con,
    # this is when they switch to parental caregiver instead of dad and mom
    pcg_lib_dep_y15 = cp6md_case_lib,
    pcg_lib_dep_y15 = cp6md_case_lib,
    pcg_lib_dep_y22 = cp7md_case_lib,
    pcg_lib_dep_y22 = cp7md_case_lib,
# ------------------
# Neighborhood Collectigve Efficacy
# ------------------
    # Neihborhood efficacy - note these are not validated/standardized because edited from original measures; lower scores indicate higher levels of collective efficacy
    pcg_hood_eff_y03 = rowMeans(select(., p3k1a:p3k1e, p3k2a:p3k2e, p3k3a:p3k3h), na.rm = FALSE),
    mom_hood_eff_y05 = rowMeans(select(., m4i0m1:m4i0m5, m4i0n1:m4i0n5), na.rm = FALSE),
    #dad_hood_eff_y05 = rowMeans(select(., d4i0m1:d4i0m5, d4i0n1:d4i0n5), na.rm = FALSE),
    # year nine reverse code p4m3c and p5m3d
    p5m3c = 5 - p5m3c, p5m3d = 5 - p5m3d,
    pcg_hood_eff_y09 = rowMeans(select(., p5m2a:p5m2d, p5m3a:p5m3d), na.rm = FALSE),
    # year 15 reverse code p6i9/k6e2c and p6i10/k6e2d 
    p6i9 = 5 - p6i9, k6e2c = 5 - k6e2c,
    p6i10 = 5 - p6i10, k6e2d = 5 - k6e2d,
    pcg_hood_eff_y15= rowMeans(select(., p6i2:p6i10), na.rm = FALSE),
    #kid_hood_eff_y15= rowMeans(select(.,k6e2a:k6e2d, k6e3a:k6e3d, na.rm = FALSE)),
# ------------------
# Home Observation for Measurement of the Environment (HOME) 
# ------------------
    # Home observational scale,note says item scores are reversed to that higher score represents a better situation excerpt for condition of surround block (4 point) all others are 2 points (0,1s). reverse coding them individually would be a pain so if I have the max sum I can add it in front to just reverse it all at once.
    
    responsivity_home_y03 = rowMeans(select(., o3t1:o3t9), na.rm = FALSE),
    acceptance_home_y03 = rowMeans(select(., o3t10:o3t14), na.rm = FALSE),
    involve_home_y03 = rowMeans(select(., o3t15, o3t14), na.rm = FALSE),
    interior_home_y03 = rowMeans(select(., o3r1:o3r13), na.rm = FALSE),
    surrounding_home_y03 = rowMeans(select(., o3p1:o3p5), na.rm = FALSE),
    exterior_home_y03 = rowMeans(select(., o3p6_a:o3p7_c), na.rm = FALSE),
    home_scale_y03 = rowMeans(select(., o3t1:o3p7_c), na.rm = FALSE),
    
    warmth_home_y05 = rowMeans(select(., o4t1:o4t8), na.rm = FALSE),
    no_hostility_home_y05 = rowMeans(select(., o4t9:o4t12), na.rm = FALSE),
    verbal_home_y05 = rowMeans(select(., o3t14, o3t16), na.rm = FALSE),
    interior_home_y05 = rowMeans(select(., o4r1:o4r12), na.rm = FALSE),
    surrounding_home_y05 = rowMeans(select(., o4p1:o4p4), na.rm = FALSE),
    exterior_home_y05 = rowMeans(select(., o4p6a:o4p7b), na.rm = FALSE),
    home_scale_y05 = rowMeans(select(., o4t1:o4p7b), na.rm = FALSE),
    
    # no subscales because used items from all three versions of home
    home_scale_y09 = rowMeans(select(., o5e1:o5e17, o5e18a:o5e18i), na.rm = FALSE),
    # home_scale_y15 = rowMeans(select(., o6e1:o6e11, o6e12a, o6e12b, o6be13:o6e17, o6e18a:o6e18i), na.rm = FALSE),

# ------------------
# Early childhood  Environment Rating Scale Revised (ECERS-R)
# ------------------
    # Early childhood  Environment Rating Scale, used Sakai et al., 2003 to get the subscales and items. i don't see the parents and staff scales here. may be in in another section somewhere. also, the e3f variables don't appear to match the scales? something about time spent
    personal_care_ecersr = rowMeans(select(., e3i9:e3i14), na.rm = FALSE),
    # parent_staff_ecersr = rowMeans(select(., e3i38), na.rm = FALSE)
    space_ecersr = rowMeans(select(., e3i1:e3i8), na.rm = FALSE),
    #language_ecersr = rowMeans(select(., )),
    activities_ecersr = rowMeans(select(., e3i9:e3i28), na.rm = FALSE),
    structure_ecersr = rowMeans(select(., e3i34:e3i37), na.rm = FALSE),
    interaction_ecersr = rowMeans(select(., e3i29:e3i33), na.rm = FALSE),
    ecers_r = rowMeans(select(., ends_with("ecersr")), na.rm = FALSE),
    ecers_scale = rowMeans(select(., e3f1:e3f7, e3i1:e3i38, e3l1:e3l11), na.rm = FALSE),

# ------------------
# Early childhood  Environment Rating Scale Revised (ECERS-R): Copilot Generated 06/18/2026
# ------------------
    ecers_total = rowMeans(select(., e3i1:e3i38), na.rm = TRUE),
    # 1. Space and Furnishings
    space_furnishings = rowMeans(select(., 
      e3i1,  # Indoor space
      e3i2,  # Furniture
      e3i3,  # Furnishings for relaxation
      e3i4,  # Room arrangement
      e3i5,  # Space for privacy
      e3i6,  # Child-related display
      e3i7,  # Gross motor space
      e3i8   # Gross motor equipment
    ), na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # 2. Personal Care Routines
    personal_care = rowMeans(select(., 
      e3i9,  # Greeting/departing
      e3i10, # Meals/snacks
      e3i11, # Nap/rest
      e3i12, # Toileting/diapering
      e3i13, # Health practices
      e3i14  # Safety practices
    ), na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # 3. Language-Reasoning
    language_reasoning = rowMeans(select(., 
      e3i15, # Books & pictures / language-reasoning
      e3i16, # Encouraging communication
      e3i17, # Reasoning skills
      e3i18  # Informal language use
    ), na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # 4. Activities
    activities = rowMeans(select(., 
      e3i19, # Fine motor
      e3i20, # Art
      e3i21, # Music/movement
      e3i22, # Blocks
      e3i23, # Sand/water
      e3i24, # Dramatic play
      e3i25, # Nature/science
      e3i26, # Math/number
      e3i27, # Technology use
      e3i28  # Diversity
    ), na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # 5. Interactions
    interactions = rowMeans(select(., 
      e3i29, # Gross motor supervision
      e3i30, # General supervision
      e3i31, # Discipline
      e3i32, # Staff-child interaction
      e3i33  # Child-child interaction
    ), na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # 6. Program Structure
    program_structure = rowMeans(select(., 
      e3i34, # Schedule
      e3i35, # Free play
      e3i36, # Group time
      e3i37  # Disabilities provisions
    ), na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # 7. Parents and Staff
    parents_staff = rowMeans(select(., 
      e3i38  # Provisions for parents
      # NOTE: Your dataset only includes 1 item here,
      # while full ECERS-R has more items (39–43)
    ), na.rm = TRUE),

# ------------------
# Material Hardship
# ------------------
  # no established scoring
  material_hardship_y01 = rowMeans(select(., m2h19a:m2h19i, f2h17a:f2h17l), 
                                         na.rm = FALSE),
# i & j only asked in some cities
  material_hardship_y03 = rowMeans(select(., m3i23a:m3i23j, f3i23a:f3i23j), 
                                  na.rm = FALSE),
  material_hardship_y05 = rowMeans(select(., m4i23a:m4i23m, f4i23a:f4i23m), 
                                  na.rm = FALSE),

# adds non-parental caregiver questions
  material_hardship_y09 = rowMeans(select(., m5f23a:m5f23j, f5f23a:f5f23j, n5g1a:n5g1j), 
                                  na.rm = FALSE),
# has past year or past six years not in the past year. here we switch to PCG
  material_hardship_y15 = rowMeans(select(., p6j37:p6j47, p6j48:p6j58), 
                                  na.rm = FALSE),
# ------------------
# Aggravation in Parenting
# ------------------

# did not implement full scale.
  mom_aggravation_y01 = rowMeans(select(., m2b20a:m2b20d, m2b44a:m2b44d, m2b20ax, m2b20bx, m2b20cx),
                            na.rm = FALSE),
  dad_aggravation_y01 = rowMeans(select(., f2b18a:f2b18d, f2b38a:f2b38d, f2b20ax, f2b20bx, f2b20cx),
                            na.rm = FALSE),
  #pcg_aggravation_y01 = rowMeans(select(., mom_aggravation_y01, dad_aggravation_y01),na.rm = FALSE),

  mom_aggravation_y03 = rowMeans(select(., m3b6a:m3b6d, m3b34a:m3b34d),
                                 na.rm = FALSE),
  dad_aggravation_y03 = rowMeans(select(.,  f3b6a:f3b6d, f3b34a:f3b34d),
                                 na.rm = FALSE),
  #pcg_aggravation_y03 = rowMeans(select(., mom_aggravation_y03, dad_aggravation_y03), na.rm = FALSE),

  mom_aggravation_y05 = rowMeans(select(., m4b6a:m4b6d, m4b31a:m4b31d),
                                 na.rm = FALSE),
  dad_aggravation_y05 = rowMeans(select(., f4b6a:f4b6d, f4b31a:f4b31d),
                                na.rm = FALSE),
  #pcg_aggravation_y05 = rowMeans(select(., mom_aggravation_y05, dad_aggravation_y05), na.rm = FALSE),

  mom_aggravation_y09 = rowMeans(select(., m5k2a:m5k2d),
                                 na.rm = FALSE),
  dad_aggravation_y09 = rowMeans(select(.,  f5k2a:f5k2d),
                                 na.rm = FALSE),
  #pcg_aggravation_y09 = rowMeans(select(., p5k1a:p5k1d), na.rm = FALSE),

  pcg_aggravation_y15 = rowMeans(select(., p6d32:p6d35),
                                na.rm = FALSE)
)
  
    
    
    #  used Sakai et al., 2003 to get the subscales and items
    ecersr <- df %>% select(., e3f1:e3f7, e3i1:e3i38, e3l1:e3l11) # running alfpha found e3f3:e3f7 reverse correlated and should be reversed.
    # view variables to create subscales
    view_df(ecersr, show.frq = TRUE)

# note, need to do the home observation scale for year 5, but they direct to hte Leventhall et al. paper instead of explaining how to construcgt the scale. note that year 5 also has parental versions of the scale ase well
    
```

# Save dataset
```{r}

df_subset <- df %>% select(math_basic:pcg_aggravation_y15) 
haven::write_dta(df_subset, "ffcws_subset_ece.dta")
haven::write_dta(df_subset, "ffcws_subset_ece.sav")
```
